Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accessions section states MN108491 for CcUGT3 (UGT703E1).

Source_Species.reason: Paper refers to montbretia as the source species throughout.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Species given as Crocosmia × crocosmiiflora in title/keywords/plant material.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material section states variety Emily McKenzie.

Applied_Species.reason: Gene studied in montbretia and function validated by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Crocosmia × crocosmiiflora and Nicotiana benthamiana are explicitly named.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Emily McKenzie is stated for montbretia; no N. benthamiana variety is given, so NA is acceptable.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT703E1/CcUGT3 is a family 1 UGT, placed in clade D, and discussed as a glycoside glucosyltransferase (GGT).

Enzyme_Name.reason: Summary explicitly calls it UDP-dependent glucosyltransferase CcUGT3.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Main subject is montbretin A biosynthesis and CcUGT3 is assigned to this pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Paper places CcUGT3 in steps 4–6, i.e. late glycosylation after mini-MbA formation in acylated flavonol glycoside assembly.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper explicitly defines CcUGT3 as step 4 of 6, but does not use the term 'midstream'; this label is interpretive.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Recombinant enzyme assays used mini-MbA and UDP-Glc and identified product formation.

Primary_Product.reason: UGT703E1 converts mini-MbA to MbA-XR2 in vitro and in planta.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Summary and Results state CcUGT3 catalyzes 1,2-glucosylation of mini-MbA using UDP-Glc to form myricetin 3-O-(glucosyl-6′-O-caffeoyl)-glucosyl rhamnoside (MbA-XR2).

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MbA-XR2 is shown as step-4 intermediate and precursor on the way to MbA; thus it forms a precursor toward the end product.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish CcUGT3 as rate-limiting; 'not established' matches the evidence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: MbA is described as an acylated flavonol glycoside.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary states MbA is a highly effective and selective inhibitor of human pancreatic α-amylase and is being developed for T2D/obesity.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: MbA and pathway intermediates are discussed in young corms; young corms have high biosynthetic activity and intermediates accumulate there.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show conversion of mini-MbA to MbA-XR2 and production of MbA-XR2/MbB-XR2 in N. benthamiana upon co-expression.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is directly supported: co-expression candidate discovery, recombinant conversion by UGT703E1 not UGT703E2, higher/peaking expression in young corms, and in planta validation in N. benthamiana.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/Methods and used to validate CcUGT3.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: qRT-PCR shows highest expression in young corms in June, over 28-fold higher than old corms, with low expression in old corms.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves under a 35S construct is explicitly described, and function was validated there.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Developmental stage (young vs old corm; seasonal time points) and Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana were central experimental factors.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These genes were co-expressed in reconstruction experiments, but the paper does not demonstrate direct protein-protein interactions with CcUGT3.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Figure 6 and Methods state 35S promoter constructs were used for transient expression.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome co-expression/differential expression were used and SRA accession SRP108844 is provided.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly presents CcUGT3 as useful for metabolic engineering of MbA and notes the need for additional UGTs and improved caffeoyl-CoA supply.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion states N. benthamiana availability of caffeoyl-CoA is limiting, favoring MbB instead of MbA.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show no activity with MRG, acceptance of UDP-Xyl/UDP-Rha with preference for UDP-Glc, and assignment to step 4 of a six-step pathway.

corrections.reason: Paper explicitly defines CcUGT3 as step 4 of 6, but does not use the term 'midstream'; this label is interpretive.

corrections.old_value: CcMYB4; CcFLS; CcCYP2; CcUGT1; CcUGT2; CcAT1
corrections.reason: These genes were co-expressed in reconstruction experiments, but the paper does not demonstrate direct protein-protein interactions with CcUGT3.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accessions section lists MN108492 for UGT703E2.

Source_Species.reason: UGT703E2 was cloned from montbretia corm cDNA.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Montbretia is identified as Crocosmia × crocosmiiflora.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material specifies variety Emily McKenzie.

Applied_Species.reason: UGT703E2 was cloned from montbretia but functionally tested after heterologous expression in E. coli, so listing only montbretia omits the experimental assay host.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: As above, the gene was sourced from Crocosmia × crocosmiiflora but recombinant testing occurred in E. coli; this field is incomplete if meant to cover applied systems.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Emily McKenzie is the only source variety stated; no other plant application variety is involved.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT703E2 is a family 1 UGT and falls into clade D in phylogenetic analysis.

Enzyme_Name.reason: As a UGT candidate, calling it UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase UGT703E2 is consistent with the paper's nomenclature.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It was screened as a candidate for step 4 in montbretin A biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Paper tests it specifically as a late glycosylation candidate for mini-MbA to MbA-XR2 conversion.

Primary_Substrate.reason: UGT703E2 was assayed with mini-MbA and UDP-Glc.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results explicitly state no activity was observed with UGT703E2 using mini-MbA and UDP-Glc.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The study did not establish a connection of UGT703E2 to the end product; 'not established' fits.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: No such role was established in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The candidate screen concerns the MbA pathway whose end product is montbretin A.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: MbA is described as an acylated flavonol glycoside.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: MbA is described as a potent/selective human pancreatic α-amylase inhibitor with anti-diabetic/anti-obesity relevance.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The pathway and metabolite accumulation are associated with young corms.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Under the tested assay conditions, UGT703E2 showed no detectable activity toward mini-MbA glucosylation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper shows UGT703E2 is a close paralog cloned from corm cDNA, lacks activity with mini-MbA + UDP-Glc, and is therefore not the validated step-4 enzyme.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Candidate cloning and recombinant enzyme assays with LC-MS are exactly what was used.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome resources and SRA SRP108844 are described as the basis for candidate identification and assembly support.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper shows lack of detected step-4 activity, but it does not explicitly discuss 'limited direct value for engineering'; that is a reasonable inference, not a stated conclusion.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper states UGT703E2 is highly similar to UGT703E1 yet showed no validated activity in this study.

Source_Species.reason: CcMYB4 is a montbretia gene used in the study.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material states variety Emily McKenzie.

Applied_Species.reason: CcMYB4 was used in transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Regulator_Type.reason: The gene name MYB and prior description as a myricetin biosynthesis gene support that it is a transcription factor, though this paper relies on prior work.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No nuclear localization experiment or statement for CcMYB4 appears in this paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Within this paper, CcMYB4 was part of the six-gene set enabling heterologous production of mini-MbA/mini-MbB precursors in N. benthamiana.

Regulation_Mode.reason: As a MYB transcription factor, transcriptional activation is plausible, but this paper does not directly test or state its regulation mode.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The MYB name supports assignment as a MYB transcription factor, though characterization was from previous work.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods/Figure 6 state 35S-promoter-gene constructs were used in N. benthamiana.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: In this paper CcMYB4 was used in transient co-expression in N. benthamiana as part of pathway reconstruction.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Its utility in reconstruction is implied, but this paper does not explicitly discuss CcMYB4 as an engineering target/value in its own right.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf accumulation in the heterologous system is supported, but direct attribution of young corm accumulation to CcMYB4 in this paper is indirect and based on pathway context, not direct results on this gene.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are consistent with the paper: it was one of three myricetin biosynthesis genes used in N. benthamiana, enabled precursor production for CcUGT3 validation, and was not newly characterized mechanistically here.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves under the 35S promoter.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The reconstructed pathway feeds toward montbretin A.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: It is upstream and enabling in the heterologous reconstruction, but this paper does not directly demonstrate a decisive regulatory influence on MbA production.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper only states CcMYB4 is among myricetin biosynthesis genes from prior work; direct control of precursor biosynthesis is not tested here.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: No direct target genes or regulatory effects of CcMYB4 are analyzed in this paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These are co-expressed pathway components, not demonstrated protein interaction partners of CcMYB4.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: MbA is described as a potent and selective inhibitor of human pancreatic α-amylase with anti-diabetic/anti-obesity relevance.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: MbA is an acylated flavonol glycoside.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that CcMYB4 was included only as part of the reconstitution system and not directly characterized in this paper.

corrections.reason: No nuclear localization experiment or statement for CcMYB4 appears in this paper.

corrections.old_value: Supports expression of upstream myricetin biosynthetic capacity in reconstruction system
corrections.reason: No direct target genes or regulatory effects of CcMYB4 are analyzed in this paper.

corrections.old_value: CcFLS; CcCYP2; CcUGT1; CcUGT2; CcAT1; CcUGT3
